title: polystichum woodsioides
intro: Polystichum woodsioides, also known as the Woods' fern, is a species of evergreen fern native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Polystichum woodsioides include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and promote overall well-being. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
